174-0 025 Email to Peter Mandelson from Rachel Reeves (Chancellor of

the Exchequer)

From: Rachel Reeves Personal ]

Sent: Sun 06/04/2025 10:28:24 AM (UTC)

To: Peter Mandelson| Personal ]

Subject: Re: MONITORING: Stephen Miller, Fox News Interview Transcript: Tariffs, Stock
Markets, Global Trade Imbalances, Europe - 4/4/2025

These are very helpful.

Sent from my iPhone

On 5 Apr 2025, at 20:45, Peter Mandelson wrote:

OFFICIAL

Get Outlook for iOS

From: JCS

Sent: Saturday, April 5, 2025 2:20:55 PM

To: Peter Mandelson

Subject: Fw: MONITORING: Stephen Miller, Fox News Interview Transcript: Tariffs, Stock
Markets, Global Trade Imbalances, Europe - 4/4/2025

OFFICIAL

JCS

Director of Communications & Spokesperson
British Embassy Washington
Personal

OFFICIAL

From: JCS

Sent: Friday, April 4, 2025 4:44:37 PM

To: BEW Monitoring

Subject: MONITORING: Stephen Miller, Fox News Interview Transcript: Tariffs, Stock
Markets, Global Trade Imbalances, Europe - 4/4/2025


OFFICIAL
White House Deputy Chief of Staff, Stephen Miller, did a Fox News 
broadcast interview with Sandra Smith. During the conversation Miller 
defended President Trump’ s new tariff’ s and the need for them “ The 
United States is 36% of global consumption, and we're more than 25% of 
the world's economy, just the United States alone. That means we get to 
set the rules and terms for international trade”  He also referenced 
Europe several times:
Stephen Miller
Our leaders allowed foreign countries to rig the rules of the game, to
cheat, to steal, to rob, to plunder, that has cost America trillions of dollars
in wealth. These countries cannot win a trade fight with us, because
they're already the ones who have cheated. So in other words, they've
stolen our industries. We have all the upside here in this fight, because we
can now make these industries come back, because we have the market.
We have the consumers they need. Japan cannot replace the American
automobile market. Europe can't replace the American automobile 
market.
They need us. And this is a fundamental issue of fairness and national
security.”
“We protect and defend all of Europe, and it costs them trillions of dollars
over time. And what do they do? They run an over $300 million trade
deficit with the United States. Why should America protect the world, send
foreign aid to the world, defend the world, provide for the world, in
exchange get ripped off by every other country in the world.”
Please find a rough transcript below:
Sandra Smith
All right. Fox news alert here, as the selling does seem to continue in the 
American stock market, you've got the Dow sinking to the lows of the 
session as we speak right now, down 2100, points at 38,418. Okay, so 
let's bring in Stephen Miller. He's at the White House. He's the White 
House Deputy Chief of Staff. Your timing is perfect, because I'm sure 
there are a lot of folks out there who would like to know the White House 
thinking here, Stephen, please do share with us your thoughts in this 
moment, and especially after China announced these retaliatory tariffs, it 
seems like that's when the markets took another leg down here. Where is 
all of this going next?
Stephen Miller
Well, let's first establish an important foundation. The United States is 36% 
of global consumption, and we're more than 25% of the world's economy, 
just the United States alone. That means we get to set the rules and terms 
for international trade. But we've done the opposite as a country for 
decades now, when we hear the term globalization, what does that really 
mean? It means taking America's wealth and shipping it to foreign 
510


countries. So all the factories that used to be in America are now in China, 
Cambodia, Thailand, Vietnam, Canada, Mexico, parts of Africa, India, 
Europe, Japan, Korea, you know where they’ re not? the United States of 
America. Because our leaders allowed foreign countries to rig the rules of 
the game, to cheat, to steal, to rob, to plunder, that has cost America 
trillions of dollars in wealth. These countries cannot win a trade fight with 
us, because they're already the ones who have cheated. So in other 
words, they've stolen our industries. We have all the upside here in this 
fight, because we can now make these industries come back, because we 
have the market. We have the consumers they need. Japan cannot 
replace the American automobile market. Europe can't replace the 
American automobile market. They need us. And this is a fundamental 
issue of fairness and national security. Tell me. Why is it that if you go to 
Tokyo, there's no American cars if you go to Berlin, there's no American 
cars, but on our streets we have every foreign car there is in the world. 
They've locked their markets to our cars, they have stolen from our 
workers and our people, and President Trump is saying that has to end. 
We need our wealth back and our jobs back and the quality of America, 
the American quality of life will rise exponentially as these policies kick in, 
and as the great onshoring and return of American wealth occurs. 
Sandra Smith
Are you confident we can replace that production, and we can do so in a 
way that Americans can actually afford, and like those cars?
Stephen Miller
without question. In fact, the changes in advanced manufacturing 
technology, 3d printing, robotics, artificial intelligence. This makes it more 
and more affordable to manufacture, produce and build at scale in the 
United States. American workers should not have to compete with child 
slave wages in Vietnam. They should not have to. That is wrong. We do 
not need a global race to the bottom that says that American workers have 
to earn the same wages and work in the same conditions as the poorest 
workers in the third world. That's the system that we've had with the right 
tax policy, the right regulation policy, the right trade policy, and investing in 
advanced manufacturing, all of these industries and supply chains can 
come back home. But here's the thing, it's not a choice. It's a national 
security imperative. What would happen if there was a war and we didn't 
have the ability to manufacture our own steel, our own aluminum, our own 
medicine, our own automobiles, our own aircraft? What would happen to 
the United States of America? It is a fundamental matter of national 
survival.
Sandra Smith
Yeah, well, we got a glimpse of that during COVID right when people 
couldn't get what they needed. We learned so much of it was being made 
outside of the United States. And how about the food industry? This was 
really interesting perspective right here on America Reports last hour from 
a shrimper. Listen.
Craig Reave (Sea Eagle Seafood Owner)
511


we've been getting killed by imports for not just years, but literally 
decades. So we've been suffering for a long, long time, and these tariffs, 
we believe are going to give us some immediate relief. We've outsourced 
our whole industry. 94% of the shrimp consumed in the United States is 
imported.
Sandra Smith
Okay so he shared with us, he's optimistic that this could help his industry 
survive, and they've seen a decline for decades now, because all of these, 
you know, all the shrimps been coming in from all these other countries 
into the United States, low quality shrimp, he pointed out, but there's. 
Stephen Miller
By the way, a lot of that, a lot of the seafood that we're importing has 
formaldehyde in it, is not safe for consumption, and this is this whole 
situation. People have to understand just how badly we've been ripped off 
as a country. You heard that gentleman, 94% of ship is imported to the 
United States, and we have the Gulf of America. The US agriculture and 
seafood industry has been decimated because we allow for subpar, cheap 
foreign imports without limit that are subsidized by foreign countries to put 
our farmers and our shrimpers out of business. Well, their markets are 
close to ours. Can someone explain to me, we protect and defend South 
Korea, and in exchange, they steal our automobile industry, they steal our 
electronics industry. We protect and defend Japan, and exchange they 
close their market to our cars. We protect and defend Canada, and they 
run up a massive trade deficit with us. We protect and defend all of 
Europe, and it costs them trillions of dollars over time. And what do they 
do? They run an over $300 million trade deficit with the United States. 
Why should America protect the world, send foreign aid to the world, 
defend the world, provide for the world, in exchange get ripped off by 
every other country in the world. What we have to show for it, the 
deindustrialization of the heartland, the destruction of the American 
Dream, the eradication of our industrial and manufacturing base that we 
need for national security and the crushing of the American dream. It has 
to stop, not to mention $36 trillion in debt.
Sandra Smith
I’ ve only got a few seconds left, but people are throwing around the R 
word, and they see the Dow down 2100 points. And people are worried 
about a recession. They're worried that we can't do this without sending 
this economy into such a deep dive that we can't come back from it. And I 
know you guys are all about growth. I know you're about lowering taxes, 
rolling back regulations, and seeing that growth. Are you concerned that 
this is going to put us into a recession?
Stephen Miller
The exact opposite. We inherited from Joe Biden, an economy in total free 
fall. There has been zero jobs growth for American workers in four years. 
In other words, all jobs growth had gone to foreign workers. That is a 
depression, not a recession. That is a depression for the American worker. 
512


We had debt and deficits that were rising unsustainably that would lead us 
into a certain debt and deficit crisis. We had inflation that was in high 
double digits, crushing the American family, making it impossible to afford 
the essentials of life. We had an economy that was in a state of calamity 
and catastrophe. President Trump's policy of reshoring our supply chains, 
pushing for total expensing of capital gains, eliminating regulation, 
eliminating intrusive green, new scam environmental rules, pushing for the 
biggest, broadest tax cut in American history, and reducing taxes on 
American workers, including ending taxes on tips, ending taxes on 
overtime, ending taxes on Social Security. This economic program is the 
single most important program for American wealth, prosperity and 
rejuvenation that we've ever seen in this country.
Sandra Smith
I’d love to continue the conversation hope to have you back on soon 
Steven, thank you so much.
Stephen Miller
Thank you
Please contact BEW Monitoring 
 with any 
questions or inquiries regarding Washington Newsteam products.
OFFICIAL
OFFICIAL

This email is intended for the addressee(s) only: All messages sent and received by the

513
parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
